pattern: The minimal subtree joining three nodes in a tree has weight equal to half the sum of their three pairwise path distances, so binary-lifted LCA answers each query in logarithmic time.
hints: A connected subtree spanning three given nodes is their Steiner tree; in a tree every edge on it is counted by exactly two of the three pairwise paths.
Therefore the answer is (dist(a,b) + dist(b,c) + dist(a,c)) / 2, an exact integer.
Precompute weighted depth and ancestors for LCA so each pairwise distance is depth[u] + depth[v] - 2 * depth[lca(u,v)].

edge_inputs.description: two queries on a small branching tree
single query on a path of three nodes
all three meet at a common branch point
sources on the same side of the destination

stress.description: random weighted tree plus random distinct-triple queries, checked by per-query BFS distances
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, size)
    edges = []
    for v in range(1, n):
        u = rng.randrange(0, v)
        w = rng.randrange(1, 10000)
        edges.append([u, v, w])
    queries = []
    for _ in range(max(1, n // 3)):
        trip = rng.sample(range(n), 3)
        queries.append(trip)
    return [edges, queries]
